evidence-type:derived.description: The value was computed from more primitive measurements on the system using a documented, deterministic procedure.
evidence-type:derived.label: Derived

evidence-type:direct.description: The value was measured directly on the system itself using an instrument or procedure whose output is the metric of interest.
evidence-type:direct.label: Direct measurement

evidence-type:expert_estimate.description: The value is a qualitative or quantitative estimate from domain experts, typically used when no direct or derived value is available.
evidence-type:expert_estimate.label: Expert estimate

evidence-type:proxy.description: The value substitutes for the target metric because the target is not directly measurable; the proxy has a known, documented relationship to it.
evidence-type:proxy.label: Proxy

evidence-type:simulation.description: The value is the output of a simulation or computational model of the system, not an observation of the system itself.
evidence-type:simulation.label: Simulation

evidence-type:unknown.description: The value kind cannot be determined from the source. Should be used sparingly and reviewed.
evidence-type:unknown.label: Unknown

metric-family:boundary-scale.description: Characterizations of a system's extent — spatial, temporal, organizational — and of where it ends. Examples: spatial extent, temporal horizon, number of nested levels.
metric-family:boundary-scale.label: Boundary & scale

metric-family:composition.description: Inventory and diversity of a system's components. Examples: component count, species richness, type entropy, heterogeneity indices.
metric-family:composition.label: Composition & heterogeneity

metric-family:dynamics.description: Temporal behavior. Examples: Lyapunov exponents, period, bifurcation parameters, autocorrelation structure, variance.
metric-family:dynamics.label: Dynamics & stability

metric-family:feedback-adaptation.description: Capacities for regulation, learning, and self-modification. Examples: feedback loop count, homeostatic range, learning rate, adaptive capacity indices.
metric-family:feedback-adaptation.label: Feedback, control & adaptation

metric-family:information.description: Measures drawn from information theory and algorithmic complexity. Examples: Shannon entropy, mutual information, Lempel-Ziv complexity, thermodynamic depth.
metric-family:information.label: Information & uncertainty

metric-family:resilience.description: Persistence under disturbance and recovery trajectories. Examples: recovery time, critical slowing down indicators, basin-of-attraction depth, functional redundancy.
metric-family:resilience.label: Resilience & recovery

metric-family:throughput.description: Flows of matter, energy, or resource through the system. Examples: mass flux, energy flux, productivity, transaction volume.
metric-family:throughput.label: Throughput & metabolism

metric-family:topology.description: Structural properties of the network of interactions among components. Examples: degree distribution, clustering coefficient, modularity, centrality, path length.
metric-family:topology.label: Interaction topology

system-class:artificial-neural-network.description: Parameterized differentiable model trained via gradient descent.
system-class:artificial-neural-network.label: Artificial neural network

system-class:atomic-system.description: Bound system of one nucleus and its electrons (neutral atom or atomic ion), whose stationary states and transitions are governed by the electromagnetic interaction plus spin-orbit and relativistic corrections. The canonical unit of the periodic table and the smallest system exhibiting discrete quantum energy levels that realize chemical identity. Distinct from `molecular-system` (once added: nuclei bonded into a multi-atom structure via shared electronic states) and from `chemical-reaction-network` (once added: population-level kinetics across many molecular species). Also distinct from physical-domain classes like `ocean-circulation-cell` and `river-network`, which describe macroscale classical fluid/transport systems rather than bound few-body electromagnetic systems.
system-class:atomic-system.label: Atomic system

system-class:belief-network.description: Structured collection of propositions held by a group, with inferential and social coupling.
system-class:belief-network.label: Belief network

system-class:bureaucratic-organization.description: Formal hierarchical structure with rule-based decision authority.
system-class:bureaucratic-organization.label: Bureaucratic organization

system-class:central-nervous-system.description: Neural tissue coordinating perception, cognition, and motor control in animals.
system-class:central-nervous-system.label: Central nervous system

system-class:distributed-software-system.description: Network of computational processes coordinating over messages to provide a service, subject to partial failures and consistency tradeoffs.
system-class:distributed-software-system.label: Distributed software system

system-class:forest-biome.description: Large-scale terrestrial ecological system dominated by trees.
system-class:forest-biome.label: Forest biome

system-class:immune-system.description: Cellular and molecular subsystem that discriminates self from non-self and responds to threats.
system-class:immune-system.label: Immune system

system-class:language-community.description: Population of speakers sharing a linguistic code, with ongoing variation and change.
system-class:language-community.label: Language community

system-class:market-exchange.description: Venue where dispersed participants transact, with prices emerging from their aggregate actions.
system-class:market-exchange.label: Market exchange

system-class:metabolic-network.description: Intracellular graph of biochemical reactions — metabolites as nodes, enzyme-catalyzed conversions as edges — that collectively realize a cell's energy, biosynthesis, and regulatory functions. Distinct from `immune-system` (organism-level defense subsystem) and from `unicellular-organism` (the whole cell as a unit) by being a within-cell functional network rather than an organism or community.
system-class:metabolic-network.label: Metabolic network

system-class:microbiome.description: Community of microorganisms occupying a defined environment or host.
system-class:microbiome.label: Microbiome

system-class:molecular-system.description: Bound assembly of two or more atoms held together by chemical bonds (covalent, ionic, metallic, hydrogen, or van der Waals), whose stationary states are governed by the coupled electronic and nuclear Schrödinger problem (Born–Oppenheimer separation in most practical treatments). Spans diatomics (H2, N2), polyatomic small molecules (H2O, CO2), and macromolecules (proteins, polymers) when treated as a single bound object rather than as a population of species. Distinct from `atomic-system` (single nucleus plus electrons, no interatomic bonds) and from `chemical-reaction-network` (once added: population-level kinetics across many molecular species rather than a single bound assembly). Also distinct from biological classes like `multicellular-organism`, where molecules are components rather than the system-of-interest.
system-class:molecular-system.label: Molecular system

system-class:multicellular-organism.description: Coordinated colony of differentiated cells with a shared genome.
system-class:multicellular-organism.label: Multicellular organism

system-class:ocean-circulation-cell.description: Basin- or gyre-scale pattern of ocean currents driven by wind, temperature, and salinity.
system-class:ocean-circulation-cell.label: Ocean circulation cell

system-class:power-grid.description: Synchronized electricity generation, transmission, and distribution network with real-time supply-demand balance requirements.
system-class:power-grid.label: Power grid

system-class:protocol-stack.description: Layered set of communication protocols enabling interoperable data exchange.
system-class:protocol-stack.label: Protocol stack

system-class:river-network.description: Dendritic hydrological system draining a terrestrial area into a common outlet.
system-class:river-network.label: River network

system-class:superorganism.description: Eusocial colony (e.g. social insects) or clonal colony whose members exhibit reproductive division of labor and are treated as the unit of selection. Distinct from `multicellular-organism` (differentiated cells rather than differentiated individuals) and from `microbiome` (diverse community without unified reproductive specialization).
system-class:superorganism.label: Superorganism

system-class:supply-chain.description: Multi-organization network of suppliers, transforms, and distributors converting raw inputs to end-consumer goods.
system-class:supply-chain.label: Supply chain

system-class:transportation-network.description: Infrastructure plus flow system moving people or goods between nodes.
system-class:transportation-network.label: Transportation network

system-class:unicellular-organism.description: Single-celled organism — prokaryote (bacteria, archaea) or single-celled eukaryote (yeast, protist) — whose complete life cycle is carried out by one cell. Distinct from `multicellular-organism` (differentiated cells with shared genome) and from `microbiome` (a community of many organisms occupying a niche).
system-class:unicellular-organism.label: Unicellular organism

system-class:urban-metabolism.description: A city treated as a system of material and energy flows, coupling human, technological, and ecological subsystems.
system-class:urban-metabolism.label: Urban metabolism

system-domain:biological.description: Organisms and sub-organism systems — cells, tissues, organs, organisms, populations; metabolic, genetic, and physiological networks.
system-domain:biological.label: Biological

system-domain:cognitive.description: Systems that perceive, represent, and reason — individual minds, language communities, scientific disciplines, belief networks.
system-domain:cognitive.label: Cognitive

system-domain:computational.description: Abstract information-processing systems — algorithms, protocols, automata, artificial neural networks, distributed consensus mechanisms.
system-domain:computational.label: Computational

system-domain:ecological.description: Systems composed of biotic communities interacting with their abiotic environment — ecosystems, biomes, biogeochemical cycles.
system-domain:ecological.label: Ecological

system-domain:economic.description: Systems organizing the production, distribution, and consumption of resources — markets, supply chains, monetary systems, firms.
system-domain:economic.label: Economic

system-domain:physical.description: Inorganic material and energetic systems — atmospheric circulations, mineral formations, solar systems, fluid dynamics.
system-domain:physical.label: Physical

system-domain:social.description: Human collectives and their interaction structures — organizations, markets, cities, polities, movements, institutions.
system-domain:social.label: Social

system-domain:technological.description: Engineered artifacts and infrastructures — software systems, power grids, supply chains, transportation networks, telecommunications.
system-domain:technological.label: Technological
